lede: A look at May 3 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Niccolò Machiavelli and James Brown, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Mexico City Metro
events.desc: Twenty-six people are killed and ninety-eight are injured after an elevated section of the Mexico City Metro collapses.

events.title: Disappearance of Madeleine McCann
events.desc: The three-year-old British girl Madeleine McCann disappears in Praia da Luz, Portugal, starting "the most heavily reported missing-person case in modern history".

events.title: Kargil War
events.desc: Infiltration of Pakistani soldiers on Indian side results in the Kargil War.

events.title: Erich Honecker
events.desc: Erich Honecker becomes First Secretary of the Socialist Unity Party of Germany, remaining in power until 1989.

events.title: Birmingham, Alabama
events.desc: The police force in Birmingham, Alabama switches tactics and responds with violent force to stop the "Birmingham campaign" protesters. Images of the violent suppression are transmitted worldwide, bringing new-found attention to the civil rights movement.

events.title: Constitution of Japan
events.desc: New post-war Japanese constitution goes into effect.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Japanese naval troops invade Tulagi Island in the Solomon Islands during the first part of Operation Mo that results in the Battle of the Coral Sea between Japanese forces and forces from the United States and Australia.

events.title: Raja Harishchandra
events.desc: Raja Harishchandra, the first full-length Indian feature film, is released, marking the beginning of the Indian film industry.

events.title: Kingdom of Kongo
events.desc: Kongo monarch Nkuwu Nzinga is baptised by Portuguese missionaries, adopting the baptismal name of João I.

events.title: 1481 Rhodes earthquake
events.desc: The largest of three earthquakes strikes the island of Rhodes and causes an estimated 30,000 casualties.

births.name: Butch Otter
births.line: American soldier and politician, 32nd Governor of Idaho

births.name: Golda Meir
births.line: Ukrainian-Israeli educator and politician, 4th Prime Minister of Israel

deaths.text: Ram Balkrushna Shewalkar, Indian author and critic
Wally Schirra, American captain, pilot, and astronaut

observances.text: Christian feast day: Moura and Timothy (Catholic and Coptic Church)
Christian feast day: Philip and James the Lesser
Christian feast day: Sarah the Martyr (Coptic Church)
